emailText:
Hi there,

I came across the Salthill Hotel website and wanted to share some honest observations. No pitch.

========================================
Website Health Check - Score: 64/100
========================================

Platform: WordPress

Issues found: 6

HIGH PRIORITY
--------------------
- 4 HTTP resources (mixed content)
  Assets loading over HTTP on an HTTPS page trigger browser security warnings.

HIGH PRIORITY
--------------------
- 41 external scripts
  41 script tags means 3-5+ seconds of load time on standard connections.

MEDIUM PRIORITY
--------------------
- 15 images missing alt text
  Alt text helps screen readers and helps Google understand your images.

MEDIUM PRIORITY
--------------------
- 18 inline style blocks
  Inline style tags cannot be cached. Moving to external CSS speeds up repeat visits.

MEDIUM PRIORITY
--------------------
- 34 broken image(s)
  Images that fail to load show blank boxes or broken icons.

LOW PRIORITY
--------------------
- Placeholder/low-quality images detected
  Some images appear to be placeholders (very small dimensions).

WHAT IS WORKING WELL
--------------------
+ Page title is set
+ Meta description present
+ Uses H1 heading
+ 44 images on the page
+ Analytics active
+ Good page size (149.1KB)
+ Clean sans-serif body font
+ Uses modern CSS layout (Flexbox/Grid)
+ Viewport meta tag present (mobile-friendly setup)
+ Uses modern rounded corners / cards
+ Uses shadows for visual depth
+ Contact form/page found on: /contact
+ SSL certificate valid ÔÇö 77 days remaining
+ HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) is set
+ Content Security Policy (CSP) is configured
+ X-Frame-Options prevents clickjacking
+ X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff is set
+ Built with WordPress
+ Contact form found on: /contact, /about, /book, /reservations, /booking
+ Built with WordPress
+ Built with WordPress
+ Built with WordPress
+ Built with WordPress
+ Built with WordPress

========================================

Your current website provider should be flagging things like this for you.
If you ever need a fresh pair of eyes or a new provider, just reach out.

Best,
David
DS Studios
Websites for small businesses in Ireland.

issues.detail: Assets loading over HTTP on an HTTPS page trigger browser security warnings.
issues.label: 4 HTTP resources (mixed content)
issues.check: Click the lock icon in the address bar. If it says 'Not Secure' or shows a warning, mixed content is present.
issues.siteHint: Visitors may get browser warnings and leave your site.

issues.detail: Alt text helps screen readers and helps Google understand your images.
issues.label: 15 images missing alt text
issues.check: Right-click an image and inspect. Check if it has an 'alt' attribute.
issues.siteHint: Blind visitors cannot use your site. Google also gets weaker signals from images.

issues.detail: Inline style tags cannot be cached. Moving to external CSS speeds up repeat visits.
issues.label: 18 inline style blocks
issues.check: View page source. Count the style tags. More than 3-4 is unwieldy.
issues.siteHint: Slower repeat visits, harder code maintenance.

issues.detail: 41 script tags means 3-5+ seconds of load time on standard connections.
issues.label: 41 external scripts
issues.check: Open DevTools Network tab. Filter by JS. Count how many files load.
issues.siteHint: Slow sites lose ~40% of visitors who expect under 3 second load times.

issues.detail: Images that fail to load show blank boxes or broken icons.
issues.label: 34 broken image(s)
issues.check: Scroll through the page. Do you see any broken image icons?
issues.siteHint: Broken images look unprofessional. Each one should load properly.

issues.detail: Some images appear to be placeholders (very small dimensions).
issues.label: Placeholder/low-quality images detected
issues.check: Do any images look like grey boxes or low-res blurs where real images should be?
issues.siteHint: Replace placeholder images with actual photography or graphics.
